description_long: This object is a weathered tree stump with a short, thick trunk rising from a patch of exposed roots embedded in a square section of earth. The trunk is broken off at the top, revealing a hollowed, jagged interior, and its bark is mottled with patches of moss and lichen, giving it a greenish, aged appearance. The roots radiate outward from the base, partially above the ground, and are covered in a mix of soil and moss. The surface of the stump is rough and irregular, with visible cracks and textures typical of decaying wood. The surrounding ground is flat and bare, suggesting an outdoor, natural setting. There are no visible branches or foliage, and the object appears to be a remnant of a once-larger tree.
description: A mossy, weathered tree stump with exposed roots and a rough, broken trunk, set in a square patch of bare earth. The surface is textured with cracks, moss, and lichen, giving it an aged, natural look.
description_view_0: The stump is seen from the front, showing the widest spread of roots and a prominent hollow at the top of the trunk.
description_view_1: This angle highlights more moss on the trunk and roots, with the broken top less visible and the roots appearing slightly more compact.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the lighter, exposed roots and a straighter section of the trunk, with less moss coverage on the visible side.
description_view_3: The trunk appears more twisted and the roots are more pronounced, with a clear view of the rough, cracked bark and mossy textures.
attribute_pot_type: No pot is present; the stump is rooted directly in a square patch of earth with no visible container.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is mostly straight with a slight natural taper and irregular, jagged break at the top, showing no deliberate bonsai-style curvature.
attribute_branch_arrangement: There are no branches present; only the trunk and exposed roots are visible.
attribute_leaf_density: No foliage is present; the object consists solely of trunk and roots.
